name: Canberra Deep Space Communication Complex
description: One of only three NASA deep space tracking stations in the world. Supports missions to Mars and beyond.

name: New Norcia Station
description: ESA's first deep space antenna. Tracks missions including Mars Express and BepiColombo.

name: Yarragadee Geodetic Observatory
description: World's most productive satellite laser ranging station. Has tracked satellites since 1979.

name: Australian Defence Satellite Communications Station
description: Primary Australian defence satellite communications facility.

name: Pine Gap
description: Joint Australian-US facility for satellite signal intelligence and missile defence.

name: Mount Stromlo Observatory
description: Hosts the Space Environment Research Centre and Australia's space debris laser tracking capability.

name: Shoal Bay Receiving Station
description: Australian signals intelligence facility supporting satellite communications interception.

name: Parkes Radio Observatory
description: Iconic 64m dish. Supported Apollo 11 Moon landing. Now used for pulsar and fast radio burst research.

name: Tasmanian Earth Resources Satellite Station
description: Receives Earth observation satellite data for land and environmental monitoring.

name: Woomera Launch Complex
description: Australia's primary launch precinct. Active since 1947. First sub-orbital commercial launches in 2024.

name: Arnhem Space Centre
description: First commercial launch facility in Australia's north. Hosted first NASA launches from Australian soil since 1995 in 2022.

name: Bowen Orbital Spaceport
description: Australia's first orbital launch facility. Site of Gilmour Space's Eris Test Flight 1 in July 2025.
